amsConflict.trayOccupied: This tray is occupied by another spool. Reassigning will deactivate it:
amsConflict.printerOccupied: This printer already has a spool loaded. Assigning a new one will deactivate it:

scan.unknownTag: Tag not registered to any spool.
scan.tagDeleted: Spool for this tag was deleted
scan.lookupError: Could not look up this tag. Please try again.
scan.alreadyActive: This spool is already active. Deactivate it?
scan.notActive: This spool is not active. Do you want to activate it?
scan.activateError: Failed to activate spool. Please try again.
scan.deactivateError: Failed to deactivate spool. Please try again.
scan.holdPhoneBlank: Hold your phone to a blank NFC tag
scan.tapDescription: Tap a spool tag to activate it, or register a new one.
scan.tagRegisteredDesc: Tap this tag any time to activate a spool or add a new one.
scan.liveDisconnected: Live updates disconnected — results may be delayed.
scan.tapOnReader: Tap a spool tag on the reader
scan.noReaderDesc: Plug in your USB NFC reader, then click below to select it.
scan.supportedReadersList: Supported: ACR122U · SCL3711 · OmniKey · Feitian · NXP · SpringCard · Bit4ID
scan.usbDesc: Plug in your USB NFC reader.
scan.placeSpoolNear: Place your spool near the NFC reader
scan.supportedTagsDesc: Supported tags: NTAG, Bambu RFID, ELEGOO RFID
scan.agentRequiredDesc: To scan NFC tags using a desktop NFC reader, please install SpoolHub Agent. The agent enables secure communication between SpoolHub and your NFC reader.
scan.agentTip1: Install SpoolHub Agent on this computer.
scan.agentTip2: Make sure the agent is running in the background.
scan.tip1: Ensure the device is plugged in securely.
scan.tapIphone: Tap the back of your iPhone to the spool tag.
scan.urlToPaste: URL to paste into NFC Tools
scan.errorNotAllowed: NFC permission denied. Allow NFC access in your browser settings.
scan.errorNotAvailable: NFC is not available. Make sure NFC is enabled on your device.
scan.errorReadTag: Could not read the tag. Hold your phone steady and try again.
scan.errorLookup: Failed to look up tag. Check your connection and try again.
scan.errorWriteTag: Could not write to tag. Make sure it is blank and hold steady.
scan.errorNoResponse: No response from reader. Make sure you selected the correct NFC reader.
scan.errorBadDevice: The selected device is not outputting NFC tag data. Select a different reader.
scan.supportedTags: Supported tags: NTAG, Bambu RFID, ELEGOO RFID
scan.iphoneDesc: Use the free NFC Tools app to write a URL to your blank tag once — after that, just tap it.
scan.iphoneStep1: Install NFC Tools from the App Store (free)
scan.iphoneStep2: Open NFC Tools → tap Read → hold your iPhone to the NFC tag → copy the Serial Number shown
scan.iphoneStep3: Paste the serial number into the field below. The URL will update automatically, copy it.
scan.iphoneStep4: Open NFC Tools → Write → Add a record → URL → paste the updated URL
scan.iphoneStep5: Tap Write / OK, then hold your iPhone to the NFC tag
scan.iphoneStep6: Done — tap that tag any time to open SpoolHub automatically. First time need to register stored spool
scan.iphoneUidHint: Paste the serial number above to generate the URL
scan.scanHintDesc: Hold an NFC-tagged spool near your reader.
scan.assignSpoolQuestion: Assign this spool to a printer?
scan.assignSpoolHint: Or keep it in your inventory for later.
scan.nrDesc: SpoolHub couldn't reach a reader. To scan tags:
scan.nrStep1: Make sure the SpoolHub Agent is installed and running
scan.nrStep2: Check that the reader is plugged into a working USB port
scan.nrStep3: Reconnect the reader, then try again

addSpool.title: Add Spool
addSpool.subtitle: Select a filament from the library
addSpool.saveError: Failed to save spool. Please try again.
addSpool.manualDesc: Fill in the spool details yourself — no NFC tag required
addSpool.nfcHintWebNfc: Hold your phone to a blank NFC tag — it links automatically
addSpool.nfcHintIos: Tap to learn how to register a blank NFC tag using the free NFC Tools app
addSpool.nfcHintAndroid: Reopen SpoolHub in Chrome to enable NFC scanning
addSpool.nfcHintDesktop: Connect a USB NFC reader and scan a blank tag
addSpool.nfcTagSetupSubtitle: One-time setup using the free NFC Tools app
addSpool.iosStep1: Download NFC Tools for free from the App Store
addSpool.iosStep2: Open NFC Tools → tap Write → Add a record → URL
addSpool.iosStep3: Copy the URL below and paste it into NFC Tools
addSpool.iosStep4: Tap Write / OK, then hold your iPhone to a blank NFC tag
addSpool.iosStep5: Done — tap that tag any time to open SpoolHub automatically
addSpool.unassignTagDesc: This tag is not assigned to any spool. Choose an existing spool without an NFC tag, or create a new spool.
addSpool.unassignTagNoSpools: All your spools already have an NFC tag assigned.

addPrinter.stepSubtitleBrand: Select your printer brand to get started.
addPrinter.stepSubtitleConnection: How does your Bambu Lab printer connect?
addPrinter.stepSubtitleLogin: Sign in with your Bambu Lab account to import your printers.
addPrinter.stepSubtitleVerify: Enter the verification code sent to {{email}}.
addPrinter.stepSubtitleCloudSelect: Choose which printers to add to SpoolHub.
addPrinter.stepSubtitleScan: Scanning your local network for Bambu Lab printers.
addPrinter.stepSubtitleLan: Enter the details shown on your printer's touchscreen.
addPrinter.stepSubtitleForm: Fill in the details for your printer.
addPrinter.connectionLanDesc: Direct connection — requires IP address and access code from the printer screen
addPrinter.connectionCloudDesc: Sign in with your Bambu Lab account to import printers automatically
addPrinter.bambuSignInDesc: Sign in to import your printers automatically
addPrinter.bambuCloudSignInDesc: Link your account to sync printers and AMS trays automatically.
addPrinter.credentialsNotice: Your credentials are sent directly to Bambu Lab's API. SpoolHub does not store your password.
addPrinter.verifySubtitleGeneric: Enter the code we sent to your email
addPrinter.codeExpiredRetry: This code has expired. Please go back and sign in again to request a new code.
addPrinter.accessCodeHint: Found in Settings → Network on your printer's touchscreen
addPrinter.signInError: Sign in failed. Check your email and password and try again.
addPrinter.verifyError: Invalid code. Check the email and try again.
addPrinter.addError: Failed to add printer. Check the details and try again.
addPrinter.registerError: Failed to register printer. Check the details and try again.
addPrinter.connectedDesc: Your printer is now linked to SpoolHub.

settings.notificationsDesc: Get alerted in real time when things happen in your inventory.
settings.enableNotificationsDesc: Send alerts when events occur in SpoolHub
settings.urlDescWebhook: Receives a POST request for each event
settings.urlDescDiscord: Paste your Discord channel webhook URL
settings.evtLowStockDesc: Spool drops below its low stock threshold
settings.evtSpoolAssignedDesc: Filament spool loaded onto a printer
settings.evtSpoolAddedDesc: New spool added to your inventory
settings.restoreFailed: Restore failed. The file may be invalid or incompatible.
settings.filamentDesc: Manage your filament database source and sync schedule.
settings.autoSyncDesc: Automatically refresh the filament database when the app starts
settings.ofdUrlDesc: URL to the Open Filament Database JSON source
settings.lastSyncedDesc: When the filament database was last successfully updated
settings.appDefaultsDesc: Configure global defaults used across SpoolHub.
settings.lowStockThresholdDesc: Spools below this weight are flagged as running low
settings.currencyDesc: Used to display spool prices and total inventory value
settings.logFilesDesc: Daily rotating log files. The last 7 days are kept. Download a file to share when reporting issues.
settings.noLogFiles: No log files yet. They will appear after the app has been running for a while.
settings.exportDesc: Download a complete snapshot of your database including all spools, printers, filaments, and settings.
settings.downloadBackupDesc: Creates a point-in-time copy using the SQLite online backup API. Safe to run while the app is active.
settings.restoreDesc: Replace the current database with a previously exported backup file. This will overwrite all existing data.
settings.restoreFromBackupDesc: Upload a .db backup file. All current spools, printers, and settings will be replaced.
settings.restoreHint: Only restore from backups created by SpoolHub. A restart may be required after restoring.
settings.updatesUpToDate: You are running the latest version.
settings.updateAvailableDesc: A newer version of SpoolHub has been released. Update your Docker image to get the latest features and fixes.
settings.directionDesc: The overall visual style — Studio (warm, rounded) or Console (cool, technical)
settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Customize your workspace and preferences
settings.defaultsDesc: Configure global defaults used across SpoolHub.
settings.autoUpdatesDesc: Download and install updates in the background
settings.releaseChannelDesc: Stable is recommended for most users
settings.lowStockAlertsDesc: When a spool drops below your threshold
settings.printFinishedDesc: When a job completes on any printer
settings.lowStockThresholdAltDesc: Warn when a spool drops below this weight
settings.autoDeductDesc: Subtract used filament after each job
settings.emptyReminderDesc: Prompt to archive a spool when it hits 0 g
settings.restoreFromDesc: Replace current data with a backup
settings.exportJsonDesc: Download spools, brands and history as JSON
